title: small, fresh, crunchy Korean potatoes Gimbap
description: a light and refreshing Korean dish featuring small potatoes, dashi-extract, served in a Korean brass bowl. this recipe makes for a great snack or light meal.
instructions: wash the potatoes thoroughly and slice them into 1/4-inch thick rounds.
combine dashi-extract, sesame oil, grated ginger, garlic, and green onions in a small bowl.
heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add the potato slices.
cook the potatoes for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until they are tender and lightly browned.
remove the potatoes from the pan and add the sesame oil mixture.
stir-fry the mixture for 1 minute, then add the toasted sesame seeds, salt, and pepper.
add the mirin and gochujang (if using) to the pan and stir-fry for another minute.
assemble the gimbap by placing the cooked potatoes in the center of a Korean brass bowl, then topping with the sesame oil mixture.
garnish with additional green onions and sesame seeds (if desired).
